mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, signals rising input costs and potential central bank tightening, leading to a compression of equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4.0% but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is strong but the Federal Funds Rate is also rising, it indicates an economy potentially overheating, prompting aggressive monetary tightening that negatively impacts equity markets over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month percentage change > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
failure_condition: If the specified conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon, particularly during recessionary periods.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months during a declared recession, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate robust consumer and business confidence, driving demand for related goods and services, which positively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a short, one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent positive growth but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production indicates a contraction in manufacturing and mining output, signaling reduced corporate activity and earnings, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month percentage change is less than -0.5%
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is rising while GDP growth is simultaneously slowing, it suggests a monetary policy error or an economy struggling under tightening conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25% AND GDPC1 1-quarter annualized growth < 2.0%
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises while GDPC1 growth slows as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and pressure corporate profit margins, potentially prompting central bank tightening and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short, one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE is below 4.5% but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances.
